methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 1 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Rivian, a public-stage transportation company, presents a valuation challenge due to structural limitations in its peer set. The transportation sector contains only four same-sector comparables, none of which satisfy narrow peer-matching criteria (same stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier). This constraint—requiring a minimum of three datapoints for reliable median-based triangulation—renders the peer-median method inapplicable. The available private comparable universe consists of five firms: The Boring Company, Einride, Harbinger, REGENT Craft, and Vertical Aerospace. No public-stage transportation peers of sufficient similarity exist to anchor multiples-based valuation.

Given the absence of applicable triangulation methods from the provided dataset, traditional valuation approaches cannot be reliably executed. The peer-median approach fails due to insufficient narrow peer density, and no alternative methods (DCF-derived multiples, revenue/EBITDA comparables, or stage-adjusted benchmarking) are documented in the available data. The current market valuation of $20.7 billion reflects $13.5 billion in total capital raised, but this alone does not establish a defensible valuation range without methodological support.

This analysis underscores a critical gap: Rivian's position as a public-stage transportation company with differentiated technology lacks sufficient comparable reference points within its peer set. Further valuation work would require either broadening sector boundaries, applying asset-based or revenue-based frameworks, or obtaining additional private comparable data with documented metrics.
